title: Native Analyzer Reverse Engineering Roadmap
objective: Evolve RevDeck toward an r2-class reverse engineering product through native analysis only, without external compatibility adapters.
non_goals: No JSON import adapter as the primary analysis path.
No full decompiler in the first native analyzer milestone.
No live debugger before instruction/basic-block/CFG data models exist.

milestones.name: Native CFG Foundation
done_when: Database persists instructions, basic blocks and CFG edges.
Native indexer creates these facts for supported fixture binaries.
Tests prove no external analyzer command is needed.

milestones.name: Instruction-Driven Xrefs
done_when: Xrefs can come from native instruction facts.

milestones.name: Native Graph Workbench
done_when: Inspector shows instruction/block provenance and confidence.

recommended_first_slice.name: M3N-A Instruction And Block Persistence
scope: Create native scanner that emits a conservative instruction stream and one or more basic blocks per function.
Persist block containment and CFG edges through existing object graph.

verification: external adapter grep audit returns no active product references
